title: Silent Session Capture
description: Create /specledger.commit slash command for auth-aware commit workflow. Fix PostToolUse hook to silently skip when no credentials or no project ID. Add dual error logging (local file + Supabase) when capture fails for authenticated users.

title: Foundational: Error Logging Infrastructure
description: WHY: US3 requires a reusable error logging module before integration into capture.go and queue.go.
design: Create pkg/cli/session/errorlog.go with LogCaptureError() function. Writes JSONL to ~/.specledger/capture-errors.log first (guaranteed), then POSTs to Supabase /rest/v1/session_capture_errors (best-effort). Never blocks, never panics. Also requires session_capture_errors Supabase table.

title: US1+US2: Slash Command and Agent Integration
description: WHY: Users need a /specledger.commit command for auth-aware commit workflow. Agent should invoke it when users ask to commit via chat.

items.item: specledger.commit.md created with YAML frontmatter
Workflow handles all auth states (no creds, creds+no project, creds+project)
Agent instruction included for chat commit requests
sl bootstrap copies command to .claude/commands/

design: Create pkg/embedded/skills/commands/specledger.commit.md with YAML frontmatter. Workflow: check staged -> commit -> check auth -> capture session (if applicable) -> push -> show summary. Uses $ARGUMENTS for optional commit message. Instructs agent to use this command for chat commit requests.
acceptance_criteria: Slash command commits and pushes in all auth states. Agent uses it for chat commit requests. Claude built-in /commit unaffected.

title: US4: Silent Skip in PostToolUse Hook
description: WHY: The PostToolUse hook spams stderr warnings for unauthenticated users on every commit. Must silently skip when no credentials or no project ID.

items.item: Credentials check moved before project ID check in Capture()
Silent return (nil error) when no credentials
Silent return (nil error) when no project ID
No stderr warnings for missing auth/project
Existing tests updated to match new flow

design: In pkg/cli/session/capture.go: Move auth.LoadCredentials() to right after tool success check (before project ID). Return result with nil error when no credentials. Remove stderr warnings for missing project ID, return silently. No changes needed in commands/session.go.
acceptance_criteria: Zero stderr output when hook runs without credentials or without project ID. Exit code 0 in all cases.

title: US3: Error Logging Integration
description: WHY: Authenticated users with synced projects need persistent error records when capture fails. Errors must be logged to both local file and Supabase for troubleshooting.

items.item: LogCaptureError() called on upload failure in capture.go
LogCaptureError() called on retry failure in queue.go
Error includes all required fields (user_id, project_id, session_id, etc.)
Local log written first, Supabase best-effort
Logging never blocks workflow

design: Integrate LogCaptureError() into capture.go (when upload/metadata creation fails) and queue.go (when ProcessQueue retry fails). Errors logged to both local JSONL file and Supabase table. Error includes user_id, project_id, session_id, error_message, feature_branch, commit_hash, retry_count.
acceptance_criteria: All capture failures for authenticated users logged to both local file and Supabase. sl session sync retry failures also dual-logged. Logging never blocks commit/push.

title: Polish: Validation and Cross-Cutting Concerns
description: WHY: Ensure all user stories work together correctly and pass quickstart verification.

title: Create errorlog.go with LogCaptureError function
description: WHY: Need a reusable error logging module that writes capture errors to both local file and Supabase.

items.item: CaptureErrorEntry struct defined
LogCaptureError writes JSONL to local file
LogCaptureError POSTs to Supabase
Local write happens before Supabase
Function never panics or blocks

design: File: pkg/cli/session/errorlog.go. Create CaptureErrorEntry struct with fields: Timestamp, UserID, ProjectID, SessionID, ErrorMessage, FeatureBranch, CommitHash, RetryCount. Create LogCaptureError(entry CaptureErrorEntry) function that: (1) writes JSONL to ~/.specledger/capture-errors.log (append, create if missing), (2) POSTs to Supabase /rest/v1/session_capture_errors with auth token. Local write first (guaranteed). Supabase best-effort. Never panics.
acceptance_criteria: LogCaptureError writes to local JSONL file and attempts Supabase POST. Function never returns error or panics. Local file created if missing.

title: Create Supabase session_capture_errors table
description: WHY: Need a Supabase table to store capture errors for remote troubleshooting by team admins.

items.item: session_capture_errors table created
All columns match data-model.md spec
RLS policy for user self-access
Indexes on user_id and project_id

design: Create session_capture_errors table in Supabase with columns: id (UUID PK auto), user_id (text NOT NULL indexed), project_id (text NOT NULL indexed), session_id (text nullable), error_message (text NOT NULL), feature_branch (text nullable), commit_hash (text nullable), retry_count (integer default 0), created_at (timestamptz default now()). Add RLS: users can read/write own errors (user_id = auth.uid()).
acceptance_criteria: Table created in Supabase with correct schema. RLS policies allow users to read/write own errors.

title: Create specledger.commit.md slash command
description: WHY: Users need a controlled commit workflow that handles auth checking, session capture, and push in the correct order.

items.item: YAML frontmatter with description field
Workflow handles staged check
Commit message from $ARGUMENTS or generated
Auth check with silent skip for no creds/no project
Push always proceeds regardless of capture
Summary shows capture status

design: File: pkg/embedded/skills/commands/specledger.commit.md. YAML frontmatter with description. Markdown instructions for agent workflow: (1) Check staged changes via git status --porcelain, prompt if nothing staged. (2) Analyze changes, generate commit message if not provided via $ARGUMENTS. (3) Commit with git commit. (4) Check auth: read ~/.specledger/credentials.json and project ID from specledger.yaml. (5) If auth+project: note session captured by PostToolUse hook. (6) Push to remote. (7) Show summary with commit hash, branch, capture status. Reference contracts/slash-command.md for auth decision matrix.
acceptance_criteria: Slash command file created with correct YAML frontmatter. Workflow covers all auth states. Agent instructions included for chat commit requests.

title: Reorder auth check in Capture() for silent skip
description: WHY: Current Capture() checks project ID before credentials, causing stderr warnings for unauthenticated users.

items.item: LoadCredentials moved before project ID check
Silent return when no credentials (nil, nil)
Silent return when no project ID
Stderr warnings removed for missing auth/project
capture_test.go updated for new flow order

design: File: pkg/cli/session/capture.go. Move auth.LoadCredentials() call from line ~288 to right after tool success check (before project ID lookup at line ~243). When LoadCredentials returns (nil, nil) or error, return CaptureResult with nil error (silent skip). Remove stderr warnings at lines 246-248 for missing project ID - return silently instead. The commands/session.go runSessionCapture() already handles nil error correctly (no output). See research.md R2 for details.
acceptance_criteria: Capture() returns nil error when no credentials. Capture() returns nil error when no project ID. Zero stderr output in both cases. Exit code 0.

title: Update capture_test.go for silent skip behavior
description: WHY: Tests must verify the new auth check order and silent skip behavior.

items.item: Test for no credentials silent skip
Test for no project ID silent skip
Test for invalid credentials JSON silent skip
All existing tests updated and passing

design: File: pkg/cli/session/capture_test.go. Add/update test cases: (1) TestCapture_NoCredentials_SilentSkip - verify nil error, no stderr. (2) TestCapture_CredentialsButNoProjectID_SilentSkip - verify nil error, no stderr. (3) TestCapture_InvalidCredentialsJSON_SilentSkip - corrupt file treated as no creds. Update existing tests to match new auth check ordering. Use table-driven tests.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover all silent skip paths. All tests pass with go test.

title: Integrate LogCaptureError into capture.go
description: WHY: When capture upload or metadata creation fails for authenticated users, errors must be logged to both local and Supabase.

items.item: LogCaptureError called on upload failure
LogCaptureError called on metadata creation failure
All error entry fields populated correctly
Error logging is non-blocking

design: File: pkg/cli/session/capture.go. At failure points (upload failure, metadata creation failure): call errorlog.LogCaptureError() with CaptureErrorEntry populated from current context (user ID from credentials, project ID, session ID, branch, commit hash, error message). Error logging is fire-and-forget - never blocks the flow. The session is already queued by existing queue mechanism.
acceptance_criteria: All capture failure points call LogCaptureError. Error entries include all required fields. Logging does not block workflow.

title: Integrate LogCaptureError into queue.go
description: WHY: When sl session sync retries a queued session and fails again, the retry error must also be dual-logged.

items.item: LogCaptureError called on retry failure in ProcessQueue
Retry count included in error entry
Queue processing not blocked by logging

design: File: pkg/cli/session/queue.go. In ProcessQueue() retry failure path: call errorlog.LogCaptureError() with entry populated from QueueEntry fields (session_id, project_id, feature_branch, commit_hash) plus error message and incremented retry_count. Fire-and-forget, never blocks queue processing.
acceptance_criteria: Retry failures in ProcessQueue call LogCaptureError. Error entries include retry_count. Queue processing continues regardless of logging.

title: Run quickstart.md validation tests
description: WHY: Verify all 6 test scenarios from quickstart.md pass end-to-end.

items.item: Test 1: Happy path verified
Test 2: No auth silent skip verified
Test 3: No project ID silent skip verified
Test 4: Upload failure dual logging verified
Test 5: PostToolUse hook silent skip verified
Test 6: sl session sync retry verified
make test passes with no regressions

design: Execute all 6 tests from specledger/602-silent-session-capture/quickstart.md: Test 1 (happy path with auth+project), Test 2 (no auth silent skip), Test 3 (auth but no project silent skip), Test 4 (upload failure dual logging), Test 5 (PostToolUse hook silent skip), Test 6 (sl session sync retry). Run make test to verify no regressions.
acceptance_criteria: All 6 quickstart tests pass. make test passes. No regressions in existing tests.
